channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: U.S. has a quarter fewer immigration judges than it did a year ago. Here's why
item.description: The continued drain of personnel from the already strained immigration court system has contributed to depleted staff morale, mounting case backlogs â...

item.title: Influencers are promoting peptides for better health. What's the science say?
item.description: The latest wellness craze involves injecting these molecules for athletic performance, longevity and more. Scientists say the research isn't keeping p...

item.title: Poll: Most say the state of the union is not strong and the U.S. is worse off
item.description: Ahead of the State of the Union address on Tuesday, evidence continues to mount that President Trump is facing political headwinds.

item.title: The owners want to close this Colorado coal plant. The Trump administration says no
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered several coal plants to keep operating past their planned retirement, part of a larger effort to boost the coal in...

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: The political implications of the SCOTUS ruling on Trump's tariffs, China urges Washington to lift tariffs after SCOTUS decision, new poll reveals wha...

item.title: What the killing of drug lord 'El Mencho' means for Mexico
item.description: Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, known as "El Mencho", was killed during a security operation to arrest him, Mexico's defence ministry has said.

item.title: Kim Jong Un re-appointed leader of North Korea's ruling party
item.description: The announcement by the rubber-stamp party congress comes as little surprise given the Kim family's grip since the 1940s.

item.title: 2/22/2026: Left Behind; South Africa's Refugees; Is That Art?
item.description: First, a report from inside the birthplace of food stamps. Then, inside Trumpâs South Africa Afrikaner claims. And, the AI art divide: Revolutionary o...

item.title: Armed man killed after entering secure perimeter of Trump's residence, Secret Service says
item.description: The suspect was carrying a shotgun and fuel can when he was killed, officers say, while Trump was in Washington DC at the time.

item.title: 2/22: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as the Supreme Court struck a blow to President Trump's tariffs, Margaret Brennan spoke to U.S. ...

item.title: How Trump will use his State of the Union address to sell skeptical midterm voters on his plans
item.description: President Donald Trumpâs State of the Union address on Tuesday is likely to be a test run of the message that Republicans will give to voters in Novem...

item.title: Violence erupts after Mexican cartel leader "El Mencho" killed in federal operation
item.description: Mexico's Ministry of Defense security forces killed the leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel, Nemesio RubÃ©n Oseguera Cervantes, known as "El Me...

item.title: Watch: Winter storm covers parts north-east US in snow
item.description: Weather warnings and travel bans are in place, as the storm causes power outages.

item.title: Briton among 19 killed in Nepal bus crash
item.description: Nepalese police say the British national was a 24-year-old man.

item.title: Maps show snow totals, blizzard warnings for major winter storm
item.description: Blizzard warnings were issued Saturday for New York City, New Jersey, southern New England and coastal communities along the East Coast.

item.title: New York City hunkers down under blizzard warnings and a travel ban as winter storm hits Northeast
As more Americans embrace anxiety treatment, RFK Jr. derides medications
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has criticized the broadening use of anxiety medications, but doctors and researchers say the MAHA movement is misrepresenting d...

item.title: How bomb cyclones form and create dangerous conditions
